steps.title: Read the circle
steps.content:
The equation is in circle form.
$$(x+6)^2+(y-17)^2=144$$
The center is $(-6,17)$ and the radius is $12$.

steps.title: Limit possible x-values
steps.content:
A point on the circle can be at most $12$ units left or right of the center's $x$-coordinate. Therefore $a$ must satisfy
$$-18\leq a\leq 6$$

steps.title: Choose the possible coordinate
steps.content: Among the choices, only $-17$ is in the possible interval for the $x$-coordinate of a point on the circle. The answer is B.
